It is one of the [[:en:100 Famous Japanese Mountains|100 Famous Japanese Mountains]]. It is part of the [[:en:Aso Kujū National Park|Aso-Kujū National Park]]. == Summary == The Kujū range consists of a dozen or so volcanic bodies gathered in a region of 13 km east-west and 10 km north-south. The mountains in the range include: *Kujū Mountains **Mount Kujū (1,787 m)- The main peak (久住山) **Mount Nakadake (1,791 m)- The highest peak in [[Kyushu]] **Mount Inahoshi (1,774 m) **Mount Hōsshō (1,762 m) **Mount Mimata (1,745 m) *Taisen Mountains **Mount Taisen (1,786 m) **Mount North (Kita) Taisen (1,706 m) **Mount Heiji (1,642 m) The Kujū volcanic group is mainly composed of [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] and [[:en:Dacite|Dacite]], which is defined as the volcanic activity above the Miyagi pyroclastic flow deposit which formed about 200,000 years ago. The north and south areas of Mt. Kujū are plateau grasslands whose main industry is dairy farming. ==Gallery== File:Kuju01.JPG|Mount Kujū from [[:en:Mount Aso|Mount Aso]] File:Kuju03.JPG|Mount Iō from Chōjabaru File:Kuju04.JPG|Mount Hōsshō and Mount Iō from Chōjabaru File:Kuju05.JPG|Mount Kujū from Narukogawa Gorge File:Kuju Mountains 02.JPG|Mount Taisen File:Kuju Mountains 03.JPG|Kujū Flower Gardens and Kujū Mountains File:Makinoto Pass - 01.jpg|Mount Kujū from Makinoto Pass with Mount Mimata on the right ==See also== * [[:en:List of Ultras of Japan|List of Ultras of Japan]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category|Kuju Mountains|Mount Kujū}} * [http://www.data.jma.go.jp/svd/vois/data/fukuoka/502_Kujusan/502_index.html Kujusan] - Japan Meteorological Agency {{in lang|ja}} * {{cite web|url= http://www.data.jma.go.jp/svd/vois/data/tokyo/STOCK/souran_eng/volcanoes/083_kujusan.pdf |title=Kujusan: National catalogue of the active volcanoes in Japan }} - Japan Meteorological Agency * [https://gbank.gsj.jp/volcano/Act_Vol/kuju/index.html Kuju] - Geological Survey of Japan * {{cite gvp|name=Kujusan|vn=282120|access-date=2021-06-25}} {{100 Famous Japanese Mountains}} {{Authority control}} {{Ōita-geo-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Volcanoes of Kyushu]] [[Category:Mountains of Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Stratovolcanoes of Japan]] [[Category:Pleistocene stratovolcanoes]] [[Category:Holocene stratovolcanoes]] }} [[sn:Mount Kujū]] [[en:Mount Kujū]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Kuju Mountains}} [[cs:Kudžú (sopka)]] [[fr:Monts Kujū]] [[ja:九重山]] [[ka:კუძიუ]] [[lld:Crëps de Kuju]] [[pt:Monte Kujū]] [[ru:Кудзю]] [[sk:Kudžú (sopka)]] [[uk:Гора Куджю]] [[vi:Núi Kujū]] [[zh:九重山]]

[[:en:100 Famous Japanese Mountains|100 Famous Japanese Mountains]] | language = [[:en:Japanese language|Japanese]] | pronunciation = | map = Japan | map_caption = Japan | map_size = 270 | label_position = | location = On the border of [[:en:Kokonoe, Ōita|Kokonoe]] and [[:en:Taketa, Ōita|Taketa]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | range = Kujū Mountains | coordinates = {{coord|33|04|54|N|131|14|24|E|type:mountain_scale:100000|format=dms|display=inline,title}} | coordinates_ref = | topo = | type = [[:en:Stratovolcano|Stratovolcano]] | age = | last_eruption = 1995 to 1996 | first_ascent = | easiest_route = }} [[File:Kuju Volcano Relief Map, SRTM-1.jpg|thumb|290px|Kuju Volcano]] {{nihongo|'''Mount Kujū'''|九重山|Kujū-san}}, located on the border of [[:en:Kokonoe, Ōita|Kokonoe]] and [[:en:Taketa, Ōita|Taketa]] in [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]], is a stratovolcano in [[:en:Kyushu Island|Kyushu Island]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]], with a summit elevation of {{Convert|1791|m|ft|0}}. It is one of the [[:en:100 Famous Japanese Mountains|100 Famous Japanese Mountains]]. It is part of the [[:en:Aso Kujū National Park|Aso-Kujū National Park]]. == Summary == The Kujū range consists of a dozen or so volcanic bodies gathered in a region of 13 km east-west and 10 km north-south. The mountains in the range include: *Kujū Mountains **Mount Kujū (1,787 m)- The main peak (久住山) **Mount Nakadake (1,791 m)- The highest peak in [[Kyushu]] **Mount Inahoshi (1,774 m) **Mount Hōsshō (1,762 m) **Mount Mimata (1,745 m) *Taisen Mountains **Mount Taisen (1,786 m) **Mount North (Kita) Taisen (1,706 m) **Mount Heiji (1,642 m) The Kujū volcanic group is mainly composed of [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] and [[:en:Dacite|Dacite]], which is defined as the volcanic activity above the Miyagi pyroclastic flow deposit which formed about 200,000 years ago. The north and south areas of Mt. Kujū are plateau grasslands whose main industry is dairy farming. ==Gallery== File:Kuju01.JPG|Mount Kujū from [[:en:Mount Aso|Mount Aso]] File:Kuju03.JPG|Mount Iō from Chōjabaru File:Kuju04.JPG|Mount Hōsshō and Mount Iō from Chōjabaru File:Kuju05.JPG|Mount Kujū from Narukogawa Gorge File:Kuju Mountains 02.JPG|Mount Taisen File:Kuju Mountains 03.JPG|Kujū Flower Gardens and Kujū Mountains File:Makinoto Pass - 01.jpg|Mount Kujū from Makinoto Pass with Mount Mimata on the right ==See also== * [[:en:List of Ultras of Japan|List of Ultras of Japan]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category|Kuju Mountains|Mount Kujū}} * [http://www.data.jma.go.jp/svd/vois/data/fukuoka/502_Kujusan/502_index.html Kujusan] - Japan Meteorological Agency {{in lang|ja}} * {{cite web|url= http://www.data.jma.go.jp/svd/vois/data/tokyo/STOCK/souran_eng/volcanoes/083_kujusan.pdf |title=Kujusan: National catalogue of the active volcanoes in Japan }} - Japan Meteorological Agency * [https://gbank.gsj.jp/volcano/Act_Vol/kuju/index.html Kuju] - Geological Survey of Japan * {{cite gvp|name=Kujusan|vn=282120|access-date=2021-06-25}} {{100 Famous Japanese Mountains}} {{Authority control}} {{Ōita-geo-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Volcanoes of Kyushu]] [[Category:Mountains of Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Stratovolcanoes of Japan]] [[Category:Pleistocene stratovolcanoes]] [[Category:Holocene stratovolcanoes]] }} [[en:Mount Kujū]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Kuju Mountains}} [[cs:Kudžú (sopka)]] [[fr:Monts Kujū]] [[ja:九重山]] [[ka:კუძიუ]] [[lld:Crëps de Kuju]] [[pt:Monte Kujū]] [[ru:Кудзю]] [[sk:Kudžú (sopka)]] [[uk:Гора Куджю]] [[vi:Núi Kujū]] [[zh:九重山]] {{wikidata link|Q6921721}}

revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Kuju Mountains]][[cs:Kudžú (sopka)]][[en:Mount Kujū]][[fr:Monts Kujū]][[ja:九重山]][[ka:კუძიუ]][[lld:Crëps de Kuju]][[my:ခုဂျူးတောင်]][[pt:Monte Kujū]][[ru:Кудзю]][[sk:Kudžú (sopka)]][[uk:Гора Куджю]][[vi:Núi Kujū]][[zh:九重山]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q6921721]] Wikidata name: Kuju Mountains [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Highest mountain on the island of Kyushu, Japan}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=geo|九重山|date=July 2012}} {{Infobox mountain | name = Mount Kujū | other_name = 九重山 | photo = Kuju-san.JPG | photo_caption = Kujū Mountains | elevation_m = 1791 | elevation_ref = {{cite web|url=http://www.peaklist.org/WWlists/ultras/japan.html|title=Japan Ultra-Prominences |publisher=Peaklist.org|accessdate=2015-01-01}} | prominence_m = 1791 | prominence_ref = | listing = [[:en:Ultra prominent peak|Ultra]]
[[:en:100 Famous Japanese Mountains|100 Famous Japanese Mountains]] | language = [[:en:Japanese language|Japanese]] | pronunciation = | map = Japan | map_caption = Japan | map_size = 270 | label_position = | location = On the border of [[:en:Kokonoe, Ōita|Kokonoe]] and [[:en:Taketa, Ōita|Taketa]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | range = Kujū Mountains | coordinates = {{coord|33|04|54|N|131|14|24|E|type:mountain_scale:100000|format=dms|display=inline,title}} | coordinates_ref = | topo = | type = [[:en:Stratovolcano|Stratovolcano]] | age = | last_eruption = 1995 to 1996 | first_ascent = | easiest_route = }} [[File:Kuju Volcano Relief Map, SRTM-1.jpg|thumb|290px|Kuju Volcano]] {{nihongo|'''Mount Kujū'''|九重山|Kujū-san}}, located on the border of [[:en:Kokonoe, Ōita|Kokonoe]] and [[:en:Taketa, Ōita|Taketa]] in [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]], is a stratovolcano in [[:en:Kyushu Island|Kyushu Island]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]], with a summit elevation of {{Convert|1791|m|ft|0}}. It is one of the [[:en:100 Famous Japanese Mountains|100 Famous Japanese Mountains]]. It is part of the [[:en:Aso Kujū National Park|Aso-Kujū National Park]]. == Summary == The Kujū range consists of a dozen or so volcanic bodies gathered in a region of 13 km east-west and 10 km north-south. The mountains in the range include: *Kujū Mountains **Mount Kujū (1,787 m)- The main peak (久住山) **Mount Nakadake (1,791 m)- The highest peak in [[Kyushu]] **Mount Inahoshi (1,774 m) **Mount Hōsshō (1,762 m) **Mount Mimata (1,745 m) *Taisen Mountains **Mount Taisen (1,786 m) **Mount North (Kita) Taisen (1,706 m) **Mount Heiji (1,642 m) The Kujū volcanic group is mainly composed of [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] and [[:en:Dacite|Dacite]], which is defined as the volcanic activity above the Miyagi pyroclastic flow deposit which formed about 200,000 years ago. The north and south areas of Mt. Kujū are plateau grasslands whose main industry is dairy farming. ==Gallery== File:Kuju01.JPG|Mount Kujū from [[:en:Mount Aso|Mount Aso]] File:Kuju03.JPG|Mount Iō from Chōjabaru File:Kuju04.JPG|Mount Hōsshō and Mount Iō from Chōjabaru File:Kuju05.JPG|Mount Kujū from Narukogawa Gorge File:Kuju Mountains 02.JPG|Mount Taisen File:Kuju Mountains 03.JPG|Kujū Flower Gardens and Kujū Mountains File:Makinoto Pass - 01.jpg|Mount Kujū from Makinoto Pass with Mount Mimata on the right ==See also== * [[:en:List of Ultras of Japan|List of Ultras of Japan]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category|Kuju Mountains|Mount Kujū}} * [http://www.data.jma.go.jp/svd/vois/data/fukuoka/502_Kujusan/502_index.html Kujusan] - Japan Meteorological Agency * [https://gbank.gsj.jp/volcano/Act_Vol/kuju/index.html Kuju] - Geological Survey of Japan * {{cite gvp|name=Kujusan|vn=282120|access-date=2021-06-25}} {{100 Famous Japanese Mountains}} {{Authority control}} {{Ōita-geo-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Volcanoes of Kyushu]] [[Category:Mountains of Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Stratovolcanoes of Japan]] [[Category:Pleistocene stratovolcanoes]] [[Category:Holocene stratovolcanoes]] }} [[en:Mount Kujū]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Kuju Mountains}} [[cs:Kudžú (sopka)]] [[fr:Monts Kujū]] [[ja:九重山]] [[ka:კუძიუ]] [[lld:Crëps de Kuju]] [[pt:Monte Kujū]] [[ru:Кудзю]] [[sk:Kudžú (sopka)]] [[uk:Гора Куджю]] [[vi:Núi Kujū]] [[zh:九重山]] {{wikidata link|Q6921721}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q6921721}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]]

Kujū are plateau grasslands whose main industry is dairy farming. ==Gallery== File:Kuju01.JPG|Mount Kujū from [[:en:Mount Aso|Mount Aso]] File:Kuju03.JPG|Mount Iō from Chōjabaru File:Kuju04.JPG|Mount Hōsshō and Mount Iō from Chōjabaru File:Kuju05.JPG|Mount Kujū from Narukogawa Gorge File:Kuju Mountains 02.JPG|Mount Taisen File:Kuju Mountains 03.JPG|Kujū Flower Gardens and Kujū Mountains File:Makinoto Pass - 01.jpg|Mount Kujū from Makinoto Pass with Mount Mimata on the right ==See also== * [[:en:List of Ultras of Japan|List of Ultras of Japan]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category|Kuju Mountains|Mount Kujū}} * [http://www.data.jma.go.jp/svd/vois/data/fukuoka/502_Kujusan/502_index.html Kujusan] - Japan Meteorological Agency {{in lang|ja}} * {{cite web|url= http://www.data.jma.go.jp/svd/vois/data/tokyo/STOCK/souran_eng/volcanoes/083_kujusan.pdf |title=Kujusan: National catalogue of the active volcanoes in Japan }} - Japan Meteorological Agency * [https://gbank.gsj.jp/volcano/Act_Vol/kuju/index.html Kuju] - Geological Survey of Japan * {{cite gvp|name=Kujusan|vn=282120|access-date=2021-06-25}} {{100 Famous Japanese Mountains}} {{Authority control}} {{Ōita-geo-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Volcanoes of Kyushu]] [[Category:Mountains of Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Stratovolcanoes of Japan]] [[Category:Pleistocene stratovolcanoes]] [[Category:Holocene stratovolcanoes]] }} [[en:Mount Kujū]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Kuju Mountains}} [[cs:Kudžú (sopka)]] [[fr:Monts Kujū]] [[ja:九重山]] [[ka:კუძიუ]] [[lld:Crëps de Kuju]] [[pt:Monte Kujū]] [[ru:Кудзю]] [[sk:Kudžú (sopka)]] [[uk:Гора Куджю]] [[vi:Núi Kujū]] [[zh:九重山]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q6921721}}

[[:en:100 Famous Japanese Mountains|100 Famous Japanese Mountains]] | language = [[:en:Japanese language|Japanese]] | pronunciation = | map = Japan | map_caption = Japan | map_size = 270 | label_position = | location = On the border of [[:en:Kokonoe, Ōita|Kokonoe]] and [[:en:Taketa, Ōita|Taketa]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | range = Kujū Mountains | coordinates = {{coord|33|04|54|N|131|14|24|E|type:mountain_scale:100000|format=dms|display=inline,title}} | coordinates_ref = | topo = | type = [[:en:Stratovolcano|Stratovolcano]] | age = | last_eruption = 1995 to 1996 | first_ascent = | easiest_route = }} [[File:Kuju Volcano Relief Map, SRTM-1.jpg|thumb|290px|Kuju Volcano]] {{nihongo|'''Mount Kujū'''|九重山|Kujū-san}}, located on the border of [[:en:Kokonoe, Ōita|Kokonoe]] and [[:en:Taketa, Ōita|Taketa]] in [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]], is a stratovolcano in [[:en:Kyushu Island|Kyushu Island]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]], with a summit elevation of {{Convert|1791|m|ft|0}}. It is one of the [[:en:100 Famous Japanese Mountains|100 Famous Japanese Mountains]]. It is part of the [[:en:Aso Kujū National Park|Aso-Kujū National Park]]. == Summary == The Kujū range consists of a dozen or so volcanic bodies gathered in a region of 13 km east-west and 10 km north-south. The mountains in the range include: *Kujū Mountains **Mount Kujū (1,787 m)- The main peak (久住山) **Mount Nakadake (1,791 m)- The highest peak in [[Kyushu]] **Mount Inahoshi (1,774 m) **Mount Hōsshō (1,762 m) **Mount Mimata (1,745 m) *Taisen Mountains **Mount Taisen (1,786 m) **Mount North (Kita) Taisen (1,706 m) **Mount Heiji (1,642 m) The Kujū volcanic group is mainly composed of [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] and [[:en:Dacite|Dacite]], which is defined as the volcanic activity above the Miyagi pyroclastic flow deposit which formed about 200,000 years ago. The north and south areas of Mt. Kujū are plateau grasslands whose main industry is dairy farming. ==Gallery== File:Kuju01.JPG|Mount Kujū from [[:en:Mount Aso|Mount Aso]] File:Kuju03.JPG|Mount Iō from Chōjabaru File:Kuju04.JPG|Mount Hōsshō and Mount Iō from Chōjabaru File:Kuju05.JPG|Mount Kujū from Narukogawa Gorge File:Kuju Mountains 02.JPG|Mount Taisen File:Kuju Mountains 03.JPG|Kujū Flower Gardens and Kujū Mountains File:Makinoto Pass - 01.jpg|Mount Kujū from Makinoto Pass with Mount Mimata on the right ==See also== * [[:en:List of Ultras of Japan|List of Ultras of Japan]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category|Kuju Mountains|Mount Kujū}} * [http://www.data.jma.go.jp/svd/vois/data/fukuoka/502_Kujusan/502_index.html Kujusan] - Japan Meteorological Agency {{in lang|ja}} * {{cite web|url= http://www.data.jma.go.jp/svd/vois/data/tokyo/STOCK/souran_eng/volcanoes/083_kujusan.pdf |title=Kujusan: National catalogue of the active volcanoes in Japan }} - Japan Meteorological Agency * [https://gbank.gsj.jp/volcano/Act_Vol/kuju/index.html Kuju] - Geological Survey of Japan * {{cite gvp|name=Kujusan|vn=282120|access-date=2021-06-25}} {{100 Famous Japanese Mountains}} {{Authority control}} {{Ōita-geo-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Volcanoes of Kyushu]] [[Category:Mountains of Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Stratovolcanoes of Japan]] [[Category:Pleistocene stratovolcanoes]] [[Category:Holocene stratovolcanoes]] }} [[en:Mount Kujū]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Kuju Mountains}} [[cs:Kudžú (sopka)]] [[fr:Monts Kujū]] [[ja:九重山]] [[ka:კუძიუ]] [[lld:Crëps de Kuju]] [[pt:Monte Kujū]] [[ru:Кудзю]] [[sk:Kudžú (sopka)]] [[uk:Гора Куджю]] [[vi:Núi Kujū]] [[zh:九重山]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q6921721}}

Kujū are plateau grasslands whose main industry is dairy farming. ==Gallery== File:Kuju01.JPG|Mount Kujū from [[:en:Mount Aso|Mount Aso]] File:Kuju03.JPG|Mount Iō from Chōjabaru File:Kuju04.JPG|Mount Hōsshō and Mount Iō from Chōjabaru File:Kuju05.JPG|Mount Kujū from Narukogawa Gorge File:Kuju Mountains 02.JPG|Mount Taisen File:Kuju Mountains 03.JPG|Kujū Flower Gardens and Kujū Mountains File:Makinoto Pass - 01.jpg|Mount Kujū from Makinoto Pass with Mount Mimata on the right ==See also== * [[:en:List of Ultras of Japan|List of Ultras of Japan]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category|Kuju Mountains|Mount Kujū}} * [http://www.data.jma.go.jp/svd/vois/data/fukuoka/502_Kujusan/502_index.html Kujusan] - Japan Meteorological Agency {{in lang|ja}} * {{cite web|url= http://www.data.jma.go.jp/svd/vois/data/tokyo/STOCK/souran_eng/volcanoes/083_kujusan.pdf |title=Kujusan: National catalogue of the active volcanoes in Japan }} - Japan Meteorological Agency * [https://gbank.gsj.jp/volcano/Act_Vol/kuju/index.html Kuju] - Geological Survey of Japan * {{cite gvp|name=Kujusan|vn=282120|access-date=2021-06-25}} {{100 Famous Japanese Mountains}} {{Authority control}} {{Ōita-geo-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Volcanoes of Kyushu]] [[Category:Mountains of Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Stratovolcanoes of Japan]] [[Category:Pleistocene stratovolcanoes]] [[Category:Holocene stratovolcanoes]] }} [[en:Mount Kujū]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Kuju Mountains}} [[cs:Kudžú (sopka)]] [[fr:Monts Kujū]] [[ja:九重山]] [[ka:კუძიუ]] [[lld:Crëps de Kuju]] [[pt:Monte Kujū]] [[ru:Кудзю]] [[sk:Kudžú (sopka)]] [[uk:Гора Куджю]] [[vi:Núi Kujū]] [[zh:九重山]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q6921721}}

It is one of the [[:en:100 Famous Japanese Mountains|100 Famous Japanese Mountains]]. It is part of the [[:en:Aso Kujū National Park|Aso-Kujū National Park]]. == Summary == The Kujū range consists of a dozen or so volcanic bodies gathered in a region of 13 km east-west and 10 km north-south. The mountains in the range include: *Kujū Mountains **Mount Kujū (1,787 m)- The main peak (久住山) **Mount Nakadake (1,791 m)- The highest peak in [[Kyushu]] **Mount Inahoshi (1,774 m) **Mount Hōsshō (1,762 m) **Mount Mimata (1,745 m) *Taisen Mountains **Mount Taisen (1,786 m) **Mount North (Kita) Taisen (1,706 m) **Mount Heiji (1,642 m) The Kujū volcanic group is mainly composed of [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] and [[:en:Dacite|Dacite]], which is defined as the volcanic activity above the Miyagi pyroclastic flow deposit which formed about 200,000 years ago. The north and south areas of Mt. [[:en:100 Famous Japanese Mountains|100 Famous Japanese Mountains]] | language = [[:en:Japanese language|Japanese]] | pronunciation = | map = Japan | map_caption = Japan | map_size = 270 | label_position = | location = On the border of [[:en:Kokonoe, Ōita|Kokonoe]] and [[:en:Taketa, Ōita|Taketa]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | range = Kujū Mountains | coordinates = {{coord|33|04|54|N|131|14|24|E|type:mountain_scale:100000|format=dms|display=inline,title}} | coordinates_ref = | topo = | type = [[:en:Stratovolcano|Stratovolcano]] | age = | last_eruption = 1995 to 1996 | first_ascent = | easiest_route = }} [[File:Kuju Volcano Relief Map, SRTM-1.jpg|thumb|290px|Kuju Volcano]] {{nihongo|'''Mount Kujū'''|九重山|Kujū-san}}, located on the border of [[:en:Kokonoe, Ōita|Kokonoe]] and [[:en:Taketa, Ōita|Taketa]] in [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]], is a stratovolcano in [[:en:Kyushu Island|Kyushu Island]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]], with a summit elevation of {{Convert|1791|m|ft|0}}. It is one of the [[:en:100 Famous Japanese Mountains|100 Famous Japanese Mountains]]. It is part of the [[:en:Aso Kujū National Park|Aso-Kujū National Park]]. == Summary == The Kujū range consists of a dozen or so volcanic bodies gathered in a region of 13 km east-west and 10 km north-south. The mountains in the range include: *Kujū Mountains **Mount Kujū (1,787 m)- The main peak (久住山) **Mount Nakadake (1,791 m)- The highest peak in [[Kyushu]] **Mount Inahoshi (1,774 m) **Mount Hōsshō (1,762 m) **Mount Mimata (1,745 m) *Taisen Mountains **Mount Taisen (1,786 m) **Mount North (Kita) Taisen (1,706 m) **Mount Heiji (1,642 m) The Kujū volcanic group is mainly composed of [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] and [[:en:Dacite|Dacite]], which is defined as the volcanic activity above the Miyagi pyroclastic flow deposit which formed about 200,000 years ago. The north and south areas of Mt. Kujū are plateau grasslands whose main industry is dairy farming. ==Gallery== File:Kuju01.JPG|Mount Kujū from [[:en:Mount Aso|Mount Aso]] File:Kuju03.JPG|Mount Iō from Chōjabaru File:Kuju04.JPG|Mount Hōsshō and Mount Iō from Chōjabaru File:Kuju05.JPG|Mount Kujū from Narukogawa Gorge File:Kuju Mountains 02.JPG|Mount Taisen File:Kuju Mountains 03.JPG|Kujū Flower Gardens and Kujū Mountains File:Makinoto Pass - 01.jpg|Mount Kujū from Makinoto Pass with Mount Mimata on the right ==See also== * [[:en:List of Ultras of Japan|List of Ultras of Japan]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category|Kuju Mountains|Mount Kujū}} * [http://www.data.jma.go.jp/svd/vois/data/fukuoka/502_Kujusan/502_index.html Kujusan] - Japan Meteorological Agency {{in lang|ja}} * {{cite web|url= http://www.data.jma.go.jp/svd/vois/data/tokyo/STOCK/souran_eng/volcanoes/083_kujusan.pdf |title=Kujusan: National catalogue of the active volcanoes in Japan }} - Japan Meteorological Agency * [https://gbank.gsj.jp/volcano/Act_Vol/kuju/index.html Kuju] - Geological Survey of Japan * {{cite gvp|name=Kujusan|vn=282120|access-date=2021-06-25}} {{100 Famous Japanese Mountains}} {{Authority control}} {{Ōita-geo-stub}} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Mount Kujū]] {{Commons category|Kuju Mountains}} [[cs:Kudžú (sopka)]] [[fr:Monts Kujū]] [[ja:九重山]] [[ka:კუძიუ]] [[lld:Crëps de Kuju]] [[pt:Monte Kujū]] [[ru:Кудзю]] [[sk:Kudžú (sopka)]] [[uk:Гора Куджю]] [[vi:Núi Kujū]] [[zh:九重山]] {{wikidata link|Q6921721}} [[Category:Holocene stratovolcanoes]] [[Category:Mountains of Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pleistocene stratovolcanoes]] [[Category:Stratovolcanoes of Japan]] [[Category:Volcanoes of Kyushu]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

Kujū are plateau grasslands whose main industry is dairy farming. ==Gallery== File:Kuju01.JPG|Mount Kujū from [[:en:Mount Aso|Mount Aso]] File:Kuju03.JPG|Mount Iō from Chōjabaru File:Kuju04.JPG|Mount Hōsshō and Mount Iō from Chōjabaru File:Kuju05.JPG|Mount Kujū from Narukogawa Gorge File:Kuju Mountains 02.JPG|Mount Taisen File:Kuju Mountains 03.JPG|Kujū Flower Gardens and Kujū Mountains File:Makinoto Pass - 01.jpg|Mount Kujū from Makinoto Pass with Mount Mimata on the right ==See also== * [[:en:List of Ultras of Japan|List of Ultras of Japan]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category|Kuju Mountains|Mount Kujū}} * [http://www.data.jma.go.jp/svd/vois/data/fukuoka/502_Kujusan/502_index.html Kujusan] - Japan Meteorological Agency {{in lang|ja}} * {{cite web|url= http://www.data.jma.go.jp/svd/vois/data/tokyo/STOCK/souran_eng/volcanoes/083_kujusan.pdf |title=Kujusan: National catalogue of the active volcanoes in Japan }} - Japan Meteorological Agency * [https://gbank.gsj.jp/volcano/Act_Vol/kuju/index.html Kuju] - Geological Survey of Japan * {{cite gvp|name=Kujusan|vn=282120|access-date=2021-06-25}} {{100 Famous Japanese Mountains}} {{Authority control}} {{Ōita-geo-stub}} [[en:Mount Kujū]] {{Commons category|Kuju Mountains}} [[cs:Kudžú (sopka)]] [[fr:Monts Kujū]] [[ja:九重山]] [[ka:კუძიუ]] [[lld:Crëps de Kuju]] [[pt:Monte Kujū]] [[ru:Кудзю]] [[sk:Kudžú (sopka)]] [[uk:Гора Куджю]] [[vi:Núi Kujū]] [[zh:九重山]] {{wikidata link|Q6921721}} [[Category:Holocene stratovolcanoes]] [[Category:Mountains of Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pleistocene stratovolcanoes]] [[Category:Stratovolcanoes of Japan]] [[Category:Volcanoes of Kyushu]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Qq]]

[[:en:100 Famous Japanese Mountains|100 Famous Japanese Mountains]] | language = [[:en:Japanese language|Japanese]] | pronunciation = | map = Japan | map_caption = Japan | map_size = 270 | label_position = | location = On the border of [[:en:Kokonoe, Ōita|Kokonoe]] and [[:en:Taketa, Ōita|Taketa]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | range = Kujū Mountains | coordinates = {{coord|33|04|54|N|131|14|24|E|type:mountain_scale:100000|format=dms|display=inline,title}} | coordinates_ref = | topo = | type = [[:en:Stratovolcano|Stratovolcano]] | age = | last_eruption = 1995 to 1996 | first_ascent = | easiest_route = }} [[File:Kuju Volcano Relief Map, SRTM-1.jpg|thumb|290px|Kuju Volcano]] {{nihongo|'''Mount Kujū'''|九重山|Kujū-san}}, located on the border of [[:en:Kokonoe, Ōita|Kokonoe]] and [[:en:Taketa, Ōita|Taketa]] in [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]], is a stratovolcano in [[:en:Kyushu Island|Kyushu Island]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]], with a summit elevation of {{Convert|1791|m|ft|0}}. It is one of the [[:en:100 Famous Japanese Mountains|100 Famous Japanese Mountains]]. It is part of the [[:en:Aso Kujū National Park|Aso-Kujū National Park]]. == Summary == The Kujū range consists of a dozen or so volcanic bodies gathered in a region of 13 km east-west and 10 km north-south. The mountains in the range include: *Kujū Mountains **Mount Kujū (1,787 m)- The main peak (久住山) **Mount Nakadake (1,791 m)- The highest peak in [[Kyushu]] **Mount Inahoshi (1,774 m) **Mount Hōsshō (1,762 m) **Mount Mimata (1,745 m) *Taisen Mountains **Mount Taisen (1,786 m) **Mount North (Kita) Taisen (1,706 m) **Mount Heiji (1,642 m) The Kujū volcanic group is mainly composed of [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] and [[:en:Dacite|Dacite]], which is defined as the volcanic activity above the Miyagi pyroclastic flow deposit which formed about 200,000 years ago. The north and south areas of Mt. Kujū are plateau grasslands whose main industry is dairy farming. ==Gallery== File:Kuju01.JPG|Mount Kujū from [[:en:Mount Aso|Mount Aso]] File:Kuju03.JPG|Mount Iō from Chōjabaru File:Kuju04.JPG|Mount Hōsshō and Mount Iō from Chōjabaru File:Kuju05.JPG|Mount Kujū from Narukogawa Gorge File:Kuju Mountains 02.JPG|Mount Taisen File:Kuju Mountains 03.JPG|Kujū Flower Gardens and Kujū Mountains File:Makinoto Pass - 01.jpg|Mount Kujū from Makinoto Pass with Mount Mimata on the right ==See also== * [[:en:List of Ultras of Japan|List of Ultras of Japan]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category|Kuju Mountains|Mount Kujū}} * [http://www.data.jma.go.jp/svd/vois/data/fukuoka/502_Kujusan/502_index.html Kujusan] - Japan Meteorological Agency {{in lang|ja}} * {{cite web|url= http://www.data.jma.go.jp/svd/vois/data/tokyo/STOCK/souran_eng/volcanoes/083_kujusan.pdf |title=Kujusan: National catalogue of the active volcanoes in Japan }} - Japan Meteorological Agency * [https://gbank.gsj.jp/volcano/Act_Vol/kuju/index.html Kuju] - Geological Survey of Japan * {{cite gvp|name=Kujusan|vn=282120|access-date=2021-06-25}} {{100 Famous Japanese Mountains}} {{Authority control}} {{Ōita-geo-stub}} [[en:Mount Kujū]] {{Commons category|Kuju Mountains}} [[cs:Kudžú (sopka)]] [[fr:Monts Kujū]] [[ja:九重山]] [[ka:კუძიუ]] [[lld:Crëps de Kuju]] [[pt:Monte Kujū]] [[ru:Кудзю]] [[sk:Kudžú (sopka)]] [[uk:Гора Куджю]] [[vi:Núi Kujū]] [[zh:九重山]] {{wikidata link|Q6921721}} [[Category:Holocene stratovolcanoes]] [[Category:Mountains of Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pleistocene stratovolcanoes]] [[Category:Stratovolcanoes of Japan]] [[Category:Volcanoes of Kyushu]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Kuju Mountains]][[cs:Kudžú (sopka)]][[en:Mount Kujū]][[fr:Monts Kujū]][[ja:九重山]][[ka:კუძიუ]][[lld:Crëps de Kuju]][[my:ခုဂျူးတောင်]][[pt:Monte Kujū]][[ru:Кудзю]][[sk:Kudžú (sopka)]][[uk:Гора Куджю]][[vi:Núi Kujū]][[zh:九重山]] [[co:commons:Category:Kuju Mountains]][[cs:Kudžú (sopka)]][[en:Mount Kujū]][[fr:Monts Kujū]][[ja:九重山]][[ka:კუძიუ]][[lld:Crëps de Kuju]][[my:ခုဂျူးတောင်]][[pt:Monte Kujū]][[ru:Кудзю]][[sk:Kudžú (sopka)]][[uk:Гора Куджю]][[vi:Núi Kujū]][[zh:九重山]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kuju Mountains]][[cs:Kudžú (sopka)]][[en:Mount Kujū]][[fr:Monts Kujū]][[ja:九重山]][[ka:კუძიუ]][[lld:Crëps de Kuju]][[my:ခုဂျူးတောင်]][[pt:Monte Kujū]][[ru:Кудзю]][[sk:Kudžú (sopka)]][[uk:Гора Куджю]][[vi:Núi Kujū]][[zh:九重山]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q6921721]] Wikidata name: Kuju Mountains [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Highest mountain on the island of Kyushu, Japan}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=geo|九重山|date=July 2012}} {{Infobox mountain | name = Mount Kujū | other_name = 九重山 | photo = Kuju-san.JPG | photo_caption = Kujū Mountains | elevation_m = 1791 | elevation_ref = {{cite web|url=http://www.peaklist.org/WWlists/ultras/japan.html|title=Japan Ultra-Prominences |publisher=Peaklist.org|accessdate=2015-01-01}} | prominence_m = 1791 | prominence_ref = | listing = [[:en:Ultra prominent peak|Ultra]]
{{ill|100 Famous Japanese Mountains|en|100 Famous Japanese Mountains|WD=Q1156761|lt=100 Famous Japanese Mountains}} | language = {{ill|Japanese language|en|Japanese language|WD=Q5287|lt=Japanese}} | pronunciation = | map = Japan | map_caption = Japan | map_size = 270 | label_position = | location = On the border of {{ill|Kokonoe, Ōita|en|Kokonoe, Ōita|WD=Q1184672|lt=Kokonoe}} and {{ill|Taketa, Ōita|en|Taketa, Ōita|WD=Q850508|lt=Taketa}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | range = Kujū Mountains | coordinates = {{coord|33|04|54|N|131|14|24|E|type:mountain_scale:100000|format=dms|display=inline,title}} | coordinates_ref = | topo = | type = {{ill|Stratovolcano|en|Stratovolcano|WD=Q169358|lt=Stratovolcano}} | age = | last_eruption = 1995 to 1996 | first_ascent = | easiest_route = }} [[File:Kuju Volcano Relief Map, SRTM-1.jpg|thumb|290px|Kuju Volcano]] {{nihongo|'''Mount Kujū'''|九重山|Kujū-san}}, located on the border of {{ill|Kokonoe, Ōita|en|Kokonoe, Ōita|WD=Q1184672|lt=Kokonoe}} and {{ill|Taketa, Ōita|en|Taketa, Ōita|WD=Q850508|lt=Taketa}} in {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, is a stratovolcano in [[:en:Kyushu Island|Kyushu Island]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, with a summit elevation of {{Convert|1791|m|ft|0}}. It is one of the {{ill|100 Famous Japanese Mountains|en|100 Famous Japanese Mountains|WD=Q1156761|lt=100 Famous Japanese Mountains}}. It is part of the {{ill|Aso Kujū National Park|en|Aso Kujū National Park|WD=Q1156555|lt=Aso-Kujū National Park}}. == Summary == The Kujū range consists of a dozen or so volcanic bodies gathered in a region of 13 km east-west and 10 km north-south. The mountains in the range include: *Kujū Mountains **Mount Kujū (1,787 m)- The main peak (久住山) **Mount Nakadake (1,791 m)- The highest peak in [[Kyushu]] **Mount Inahoshi (1,774 m) **Mount Hōsshō (1,762 m) **Mount Mimata (1,745 m) *Taisen Mountains **Mount Taisen (1,786 m) **Mount North (Kita) Taisen (1,706 m) **Mount Heiji (1,642 m) The Kujū volcanic group is mainly composed of {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=Andesite}} and {{ill|Dacite|en|Dacite|WD=Q466481|lt=Dacite}}, which is defined as the volcanic activity above the Miyagi pyroclastic flow deposit which formed about 200,000 years ago. The north and south areas of Mt. Kujū are plateau grasslands whose main industry is dairy farming. ==Gallery== File:Kuju01.JPG|Mount Kujū from {{ill|Mount Aso|en|Mount Aso|WD=Q733710|lt=Mount Aso}} File:Kuju03.JPG|Mount Iō from Chōjabaru File:Kuju04.JPG|Mount Hōsshō and Mount Iō from Chōjabaru File:Kuju05.JPG|Mount Kujū from Narukogawa Gorge File:Kuju Mountains 02.JPG|Mount Taisen File:Kuju Mountains 03.JPG|Kujū Flower Gardens and Kujū Mountains File:Makinoto Pass - 01.jpg|Mount Kujū from Makinoto Pass with Mount Mimata on the right ==See also== * [[:en:List of Ultras of Japan|List of Ultras of Japan]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category|Kuju Mountains|Mount Kujū}} * [http://www.data.jma.go.jp/svd/vois/data/fukuoka/502_Kujusan/502_index.html Kujusan] - Japan Meteorological Agency {{in lang|ja}} * {{cite web|url= http://www.data.jma.go.jp/svd/vois/data/tokyo/STOCK/souran_eng/volcanoes/083_kujusan.pdf |title=Kujusan: National catalogue of the active volcanoes in Japan }} - Japan Meteorological Agency * [https://gbank.gsj.jp/volcano/Act_Vol/kuju/index.html Kuju] - Geological Survey of Japan * {{cite gvp|name=Kujusan|vn=282120|access-date=2021-06-25}} {{100 Famous Japanese Mountains}} {{Authority control}} {{Ōita-geo-stub}} [[en:Mount Kujū]] {{Commons category|Kuju Mountains}} [[cs:Kudžú (sopka)]] [[fr:Monts Kujū]] [[ja:九重山]] [[ka:კუძიუ]] [[lld:Crëps de Kuju]] [[pt:Monte Kujū]] [[ru:Кудзю]] [[sk:Kudžú (sopka)]] [[uk:Гора Куджю]] [[vi:Núi Kujū]] [[zh:九重山]] {{wikidata link|Q6921721}} [[Category:Holocene stratovolcanoes]] [[Category:Mountains of Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Pleistocene stratovolcanoes]] [[Category:Stratovolcanoes of Japan]] [[Category:Volcanoes of Kyushu]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

{{ill|100 Famous Japanese Mountains|en|100 Famous Japanese Mountains|WD=Q1156761|lt=100 Famous Japanese Mountains}} | language = {{ill|Japanese language|en|Japanese language|WD=Q5287|lt=Japanese}} | pronunciation = | map = Japan | map_caption = Japan | map_size = 270 | label_position = | location = On the border of {{ill|Kokonoe, Ōita|en|Kokonoe, Ōita|WD=Q1184672|lt=Kokonoe}} and {{ill|Taketa, Ōita|en|Taketa, Ōita|WD=Q850508|lt=Taketa}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | range = Kujū Mountains | coordinates = {{coord|33|04|54|N|131|14|24|E|type:mountain_scale:100000|format=dms|display=inline,title}} | coordinates_ref = | topo = | type = {{ill|Stratovolcano|en|Stratovolcano|WD=Q169358|lt=Stratovolcano}} | age = | last_eruption = 1995 to 1996 | first_ascent = | easiest_route = }} [[File:Kuju Volcano Relief Map, SRTM-1.jpg|thumb|290px|Kuju Volcano]] {{nihongo|'''Mount Kujū'''|九重山|Kujū-san}}, located on the border of {{ill|Kokonoe, Ōita|en|Kokonoe, Ōita|WD=Q1184672|lt=Kokonoe}} and {{ill|Taketa, Ōita|en|Taketa, Ōita|WD=Q850508|lt=Taketa}} in {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, is a stratovolcano in [[:en:Kyushu Island|Kyushu Island]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, with a summit elevation of {{Convert|1791|m|ft|0}}. It is one of the {{ill|100 Famous Japanese Mountains|en|100 Famous Japanese Mountains|WD=Q1156761|lt=100 Famous Japanese Mountains}}. It is part of the {{ill|Aso Kujū National Park|en|Aso Kujū National Park|WD=Q1156555|lt=Aso-Kujū National Park}}. == Summary == The Kujū range consists of a dozen or so volcanic bodies gathered in a region of 13 km east-west and 10 km north-south. The mountains in the range include: *Kujū Mountains **Mount Kujū (1,787 m)- The main peak (久住山) **Mount Nakadake (1,791 m)- The highest peak in [[Kyushu]] **Mount Inahoshi (1,774 m) **Mount Hōsshō (1,762 m) **Mount Mimata (1,745 m) *Taisen Mountains **Mount Taisen (1,786 m) **Mount North (Kita) Taisen (1,706 m) **Mount Heiji (1,642 m) The Kujū volcanic group is mainly composed of {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=Andesite}} and {{ill|Dacite|en|Dacite|WD=Q466481|lt=Dacite}}, which is defined as the volcanic activity above the Miyagi pyroclastic flow deposit which formed about 200,000 years ago. The north and south areas of Mt. Kujū are plateau grasslands whose main industry is dairy farming. ==Gallery== File:Kuju01.JPG|Mount Kujū from {{ill|Mount Aso|en|Mount Aso|WD=Q733710|lt=Mount Aso}} File:Kuju03.JPG|Mount Iō from Chōjabaru File:Kuju04.JPG|Mount Hōsshō and Mount Iō from Chōjabaru File:Kuju05.JPG|Mount Kujū from Narukogawa Gorge File:Kuju Mountains 02.JPG|Mount Taisen File:Kuju Mountains 03.JPG|Kujū Flower Gardens and Kujū Mountains File:Makinoto Pass - 01.jpg|Mount Kujū from Makinoto Pass with Mount Mimata on the right ==See also== * [[:en:List of Ultras of Japan|List of Ultras of Japan]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category|Kuju Mountains|Mount Kujū}} * [http://www.data.jma.go.jp/svd/vois/data/fukuoka/502_Kujusan/502_index.html Kujusan] - Japan Meteorological Agency {{in lang|ja}} * {{cite web|url= http://www.data.jma.go.jp/svd/vois/data/tokyo/STOCK/souran_eng/volcanoes/083_kujusan.pdf |title=Kujusan: National catalogue of the active volcanoes in Japan }} - Japan Meteorological Agency * [https://gbank.gsj.jp/volcano/Act_Vol/kuju/index.html Kuju] - Geological Survey of Japan * {{cite gvp|name=Kujusan|vn=282120|access-date=2021-06-25}} {{100 Famous Japanese Mountains}} {{Authority control}} {{Ōita-geo-stub}} [[en:Mount Kujū]] {{Commons category|Kuju Mountains}} [[cs:Kudžú (sopka)]] [[fr:Monts Kujū]] [[ja:九重山]] [[ka:კუძიუ]] [[lld:Crëps de Kuju]] [[pt:Monte Kujū]] [[ru:Кудзю]] [[sk:Kudžú (sopka)]] [[uk:Гора Куджю]] [[vi:Núi Kujū]] [[zh:九重山]] {{wikidata link|Q6921721}} [[Category:Holocene stratovolcanoes]] [[Category:Mountains of Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Pleistocene stratovolcanoes]] [[Category:Stratovolcanoes of Japan]] [[Category:Volcanoes of Kyushu]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

The north and south areas of Mt. Kujū are plateau grasslands whose main industry is dairy farming. ==Gallery== File:Kuju01.JPG|Mount Kujū from {{ill|Mount Aso|en|Mount Aso|WD=Q733710|lt=Mount Aso|1=Mount Aso}} File:Kuju03.JPG|Mount Iō from Chōjabaru File:Kuju04.JPG|Mount Hōsshō and Mount Iō from Chōjabaru File:Kuju05.JPG|Mount Kujū from Narukogawa Gorge File:Kuju Mountains 02.JPG|Mount Taisen File:Kuju Mountains 03.JPG|Kujū Flower Gardens and Kujū Mountains File:Makinoto Pass - 01.jpg|Mount Kujū from Makinoto Pass with Mount Mimata on the right ==See also== * [[:en:List of Ultras of Japan|List of Ultras of Japan]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category|Kuju Mountains|Mount Kujū}} * [http://www.data.jma.go.jp/svd/vois/data/fukuoka/502_Kujusan/502_index.html Kujusan] - Japan Meteorological Agency {{in lang|ja}} * {{cite web|url= http://www.data.jma.go.jp/svd/vois/data/tokyo/STOCK/souran_eng/volcanoes/083_kujusan.pdf |title=Kujusan: National catalogue of the active volcanoes in Japan }} - Japan Meteorological Agency * [https://gbank.gsj.jp/volcano/Act_Vol/kuju/index.html Kuju] - Geological Survey of Japan * {{cite gvp|name=Kujusan|vn=282120|access-date=2021-06-25}} {{100 Famous Japanese Mountains}} {{Authority control}} {{Ōita-geo-stub}} [[en:Mount Kujū]] {{Commons category|Kuju Mountains}} [[cs:Kudžú (sopka)]] [[fr:Monts Kujū]] [[ja:九重山]] [[ka:კუძიუ]] [[lld:Crëps de Kuju]] [[pt:Monte Kujū]] [[ru:Кудзю]] [[sk:Kudžú (sopka)]] [[uk:Гора Куджю]] [[vi:Núi Kujū]] [[zh:九重山]] {{wikidata link|Q6921721}} [[Category:Holocene stratovolcanoes]] [[Category:Mountains of Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Pleistocene stratovolcanoes]] [[Category:Stratovolcanoes of Japan]] [[Category:Volcanoes of Kyushu]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages]]

{{ill|100 Famous Japanese Mountains|en|100 Famous Japanese Mountains|WD=Q1156761|lt=100 Famous Japanese Mountains|1=100 Famous Japanese Mountains}} | language = {{ill|Japanese language|en|Japanese language|WD=Q5287|lt=Japanese|1=Japanese language}} | pronunciation = | map = Japan | map_caption = Japan | map_size = 270 | label_position = | location = On the border of {{ill|Kokonoe, Ōita|en|Kokonoe, Ōita|WD=Q1184672|lt=Kokonoe|1=Kokonoe, Ōita}} and {{ill|Taketa, Ōita|en|Taketa, Ōita|WD=Q850508|lt=Taketa|1=Taketa, Ōita}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita|1=Ōita Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | range = Kujū Mountains | coordinates = {{coord|33|04|54|N|131|14|24|E|type:mountain_scale:100000|format=dms|display=inline,title}} | coordinates_ref = | topo = | type = {{ill|Stratovolcano|en|Stratovolcano|WD=Q169358|lt=Stratovolcano|1=Stratovolcano}} | age = | last_eruption = 1995 to 1996 | first_ascent = | easiest_route = }} [[File:Kuju Volcano Relief Map, SRTM-1.jpg|thumb|290px|Kuju Volcano]] {{nihongo|'''Mount Kujū'''|九重山|Kujū-san}}, located on the border of {{ill|Kokonoe, Ōita|en|Kokonoe, Ōita|WD=Q1184672|lt=Kokonoe|1=Kokonoe, Ōita}} and {{ill|Taketa, Ōita|en|Taketa, Ōita|WD=Q850508|lt=Taketa|1=Taketa, Ōita}} in {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, is a stratovolcano in [[:en:Kyushu Island|Kyushu Island]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, with a summit elevation of {{Convert|1791|m|ft|0}}. It is one of the {{ill|100 Famous Japanese Mountains|en|100 Famous Japanese Mountains|WD=Q1156761|lt=100 Famous Japanese Mountains|1=100 Famous Japanese Mountains}}. It is part of the {{ill|Aso Kujū National Park|en|Aso Kujū National Park|WD=Q1156555|lt=Aso-Kujū National Park|1=Aso Kujū National Park}}. == Summary == The Kujū range consists of a dozen or so volcanic bodies gathered in a region of 13 km east-west and 10 km north-south. The mountains in the range include: *Kujū Mountains **Mount Kujū (1,787 m)- The main peak (久住山) **Mount Nakadake (1,791 m)- The highest peak in [[Kyushu]] **Mount Inahoshi (1,774 m) **Mount Hōsshō (1,762 m) **Mount Mimata (1,745 m) *Taisen Mountains **Mount Taisen (1,786 m) **Mount North (Kita) Taisen (1,706 m) **Mount Heiji (1,642 m) The Kujū volcanic group is mainly composed of {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=Andesite|1=Andesite}} and {{ill|Dacite|en|Dacite|WD=Q466481|lt=Dacite|1=Dacite}}, which is defined as the volcanic activity above the Miyagi pyroclastic flow deposit which formed about 200,000 years ago. The north and south areas of Mt. Kujū are plateau grasslands whose main industry is dairy farming. ==Gallery== File:Kuju01.JPG|Mount Kujū from {{ill|Mount Aso|en|Mount Aso|WD=Q733710|lt=Mount Aso|1=Mount Aso}} File:Kuju03.JPG|Mount Iō from Chōjabaru File:Kuju04.JPG|Mount Hōsshō and Mount Iō from Chōjabaru File:Kuju05.JPG|Mount Kujū from Narukogawa Gorge File:Kuju Mountains 02.JPG|Mount Taisen File:Kuju Mountains 03.JPG|Kujū Flower Gardens and Kujū Mountains File:Makinoto Pass - 01.jpg|Mount Kujū from Makinoto Pass with Mount Mimata on the right ==See also== * [[:en:List of Ultras of Japan|List of Ultras of Japan]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category|Kuju Mountains|Mount Kujū}} * [http://www.data.jma.go.jp/svd/vois/data/fukuoka/502_Kujusan/502_index.html Kujusan] - Japan Meteorological Agency {{in lang|ja}} * {{cite web|url= http://www.data.jma.go.jp/svd/vois/data/tokyo/STOCK/souran_eng/volcanoes/083_kujusan.pdf |title=Kujusan: National catalogue of the active volcanoes in Japan }} - Japan Meteorological Agency * [https://gbank.gsj.jp/volcano/Act_Vol/kuju/index.html Kuju] - Geological Survey of Japan * {{cite gvp|name=Kujusan|vn=282120|access-date=2021-06-25}} {{100 Famous Japanese Mountains}} {{Authority control}} {{Ōita-geo-stub}} [[en:Mount Kujū]] {{Commons category|Kuju Mountains}} [[cs:Kudžú (sopka)]] [[fr:Monts Kujū]] [[ja:九重山]] [[ka:კუძიუ]] [[lld:Crëps de Kuju]] [[pt:Monte Kujū]] [[ru:Кудзю]] [[sk:Kudžú (sopka)]] [[uk:Гора Куджю]] [[vi:Núi Kujū]] [[zh:九重山]] {{wikidata link|Q6921721}} [[Category:Holocene stratovolcanoes]] [[Category:Mountains of Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Pleistocene stratovolcanoes]] [[Category:Stratovolcanoes of Japan]] [[Category:Volcanoes of Kyushu]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages]]

{{ill|100 Famous Japanese Mountains|en|100 Famous Japanese Mountains|WD=Q1156761|lt=100 Famous Japanese Mountains|1=100 Famous Japanese Mountains}} | language = {{ill|Japanese language|en|Japanese language|WD=Q5287|lt=Japanese|1=Japanese language}} | pronunciation = | map = Japan | map_caption = Japan | map_size = 270 | label_position = | location = On the border of {{ill|Kokonoe, Ōita|en|Kokonoe, Ōita|WD=Q1184672|lt=Kokonoe|1=Kokonoe, Ōita}} and {{ill|Taketa, Ōita|en|Taketa, Ōita|WD=Q850508|lt=Taketa|1=Taketa, Ōita}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita|1=Ōita Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | range = Kujū Mountains | coordinates = {{coord|33|04|54|N|131|14|24|E|type:mountain_scale:100000|format=dms|display=inline,title}} | coordinates_ref = | topo = | type = {{ill|Stratovolcano|en|Stratovolcano|WD=Q169358|lt=Stratovolcano|1=Stratovolcano}} | age = | last_eruption = 1995 to 1996 | first_ascent = | easiest_route = }} [[File:Kuju Volcano Relief Map, SRTM-1.jpg|thumb|290px|Kuju Volcano]] {{nihongo|'''Mount Kujū'''|九重山|Kujū-san}}, located on the border of {{ill|Kokonoe, Ōita|en|Kokonoe, Ōita|WD=Q1184672|lt=Kokonoe|1=Kokonoe, Ōita}} and {{ill|Taketa, Ōita|en|Taketa, Ōita|WD=Q850508|lt=Taketa|1=Taketa, Ōita}} in {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, is a stratovolcano in [[:en:Kyushu Island|Kyushu Island]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, with a summit elevation of {{Convert|1791|m|ft|0}}. It is one of the {{ill|100 Famous Japanese Mountains|en|100 Famous Japanese Mountains|WD=Q1156761|lt=100 Famous Japanese Mountains|1=100 Famous Japanese Mountains}}. It is part of the {{ill|Aso Kujū National Park|en|Aso Kujū National Park|WD=Q1156555|lt=Aso-Kujū National Park|1=Aso Kujū National Park}}. == Summary == The Kujū range consists of a dozen or so volcanic bodies gathered in a region of 13 km east-west and 10 km north-south. The mountains in the range include: *Kujū Mountains **Mount Kujū (1,787 m)- The main peak (久住山) **Mount Nakadake (1,791 m)- The highest peak in [[Kyushu]] **Mount Inahoshi (1,774 m) **Mount Hōsshō (1,762 m) **Mount Mimata (1,745 m) *Taisen Mountains **Mount Taisen (1,786 m) **Mount North (Kita) Taisen (1,706 m) **Mount Heiji (1,642 m) The Kujū volcanic group is mainly composed of {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=Andesite|1=Andesite}} and {{ill|Dacite|en|Dacite|WD=Q466481|lt=Dacite|1=Dacite}}, which is defined as the volcanic activity above the Miyagi pyroclastic flow deposit which formed about 200,000 years ago. The north and south areas of Mt. Kujū are plateau grasslands whose main industry is dairy farming. ==Gallery== File:Kuju01.JPG|Mount Kujū from {{ill|Mount Aso|en|Mount Aso|WD=Q733710|lt=Mount Aso|1=Mount Aso}} File:Kuju03.JPG|Mount Iō from Chōjabaru File:Kuju04.JPG|Mount Hōsshō and Mount Iō from Chōjabaru File:Kuju05.JPG|Mount Kujū from Narukogawa Gorge File:Kuju Mountains 02.JPG|Mount Taisen File:Kuju Mountains 03.JPG|Kujū Flower Gardens and Kujū Mountains File:Makinoto Pass - 01.jpg|Mount Kujū from Makinoto Pass with Mount Mimata on the right ==See also== * [[:en:List of Ultras of Japan|List of Ultras of Japan]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category|Kuju Mountains|Mount Kujū}} * [http://www.data.jma.go.jp/svd/vois/data/fukuoka/502_Kujusan/502_index.html Kujusan] - Japan Meteorological Agency {{in lang|ja}} * {{cite web|url= http://www.data.jma.go.jp/svd/vois/data/tokyo/STOCK/souran_eng/volcanoes/083_kujusan.pdf |title=Kujusan: National catalogue of the active volcanoes in Japan }} - Japan Meteorological Agency * [https://gbank.gsj.jp/volcano/Act_Vol/kuju/index.html Kuju] - Geological Survey of Japan * {{cite gvp|name=Kujusan|vn=282120|access-date=2021-06-25}} {{100 Famous Japanese Mountains}} {{Authority control}} {{Ōita-geo-stub}} [[en:Mount Kujū]] {{Commons category|Kuju Mountains}} [[cs:Kudžú (sopka)]] [[fr:Monts Kujū]] [[ja:九重山]] [[ka:კუძიუ]] [[lld:Crëps de Kuju]] [[pt:Monte Kujū]] [[ru:Кудзю]] [[sk:Kudžú (sopka)]] [[uk:Гора Куджю]] [[vi:Núi Kujū]] [[zh:九重山]] {{wikidata link|Q6921721}} [[Category:Holocene stratovolcanoes]] [[Category:Mountains of Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Pleistocene stratovolcanoes]] [[Category:Stratovolcanoes of Japan]] [[Category:Volcanoes of Kyushu]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
